name: Sage White Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 fresh winter savory or thyme sprigs
1 medium yellow onion, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the beans in a saucepan with the bay leaf, savory, and two of the sage leaves. Warm gently until ready to serve.
2. Remove herbs and bay leaf; keep beans in their broth.
3. Chop remaining sage leaves.
4. Water-saute onion for 10 minutes (add water if sautéing in water next time).
5. Add garlic, chopped sage, salt, and pepper. Cook for a minute or two.
6. Stir in wine and cook until pan is nearly dry.
7. Combine beans, broth, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and pepper. Lower heat and cook for 20 minutes, adding water if needed to keep the sauce thick.
8. Season with additional salt and pepper before serving.
9. Sprinkle parsley just before serving.
